name: Christmas Savoury Strudel
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a fairly large saucepan. Add the onions and cook them, with the lid on the pan, for about 10 minutes, until they are tender but not browned.
2. Add the garlic, the tomatoes together with their liquid, the basil, and the wine. Let the mixture simmer gently without a lid on the pan, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has disappeared and it is quite thick—this will take about 20 minutes.
3. Add the mushrooms and cook for a further 5 minutes or so, or until the mushrooms are tender and any liquid they make has boiled away. Remove from the heat and season to taste.
4. When the filling mixture has cooled, assemble the strudel on a large baking sheet. Set the oven to 200°C/400°F/Gas Mark 6.
5. Lay two sheets of filo pastry side by side, overlapping slightly where they join (if using larger squares, lay one sheet and brush with butter before adding another). Sprinkle with about a third of the nuts, then brush with butter again.
6. Repeat layers until all the pastry is used, finishing with a final layer brushed with butter.
7. Spread the tomato filling mixture over the edges, fold the edges over to enclose the filling, and roll up the strudel tightly like a Swiss Roll.
8. Brush the top with melted butter and garnish with shreds or shapes of filo pastry.
9. Bake for 30 minutes, or until golden brown. Transfer carefully to a serving dish using two fish slices.
